steps.title: Square t for use in h(x)
steps.content:
The function $h(x)$ contains an $x^{2}$ term, so we compute:
$$t^{2} = (\sqrt{54})^{2} = 54$$

steps.title: Substitute into h and solve for the answer
steps.content:
With $x = \sqrt{54}$ (so $x^{2} = 54$), substitute into $h(x) = \frac{x^{2}}{2} + 4$:
$$h(\sqrt{54}) = \frac{54}{2} + 4 = 27 + 4 = 31$$
This matches choice C — C is correct.
